title: Can You Trust IRS to Keep Your Tax Data Secure?
summary: "There have been some stories of hacks against IRS databases in the past. I think that the IRS has been hacked even more than is publicly reported, either because the government is keeping the attacks secret or because it doesn't even realize it's been attacked."
body: Monday is Tax Day. Many of us are thinking about our taxes. Are they too high or too low? What's our money being spent on? Do we have a government worth paying for? I'm not here to answer any of those questions — I'm here to give you something else to think about. In addition to sending the IRS your money, you're also sending them your data. It's a lot of highly personal financial data, so it's sensitive and important information. Is that data secure?
